articles.title: Gold (XAU/USD) stalls at critical $4900/oz resistance as bear signal flashes
articles.expand: Gold's "war premium" is fading after the US-Iran ceasefire sparked a rally, now stalled near $4900 resistance. The metal faces binary risks: a truce failure could send it to $4500, while strong net-long sentiment suggests a near-term slide. Dollar softness and silver's 7% rally provide underlying support.

The price was above a lower upward sloping trend line, and below a downward sloping trend line. articles.title: What's priced in for the Federal Reserve and ECB after the Iran ceasefire
articles.expand: The focus is hopefully going to shift back to the real economy now and that will include tomorrow's PCE report and Friday's CPI. During the time when the war dominated headlines, the news on the US economy was mostly good, highlighting that the economy was improving in February before bombs started falling. articles.title: Canada: Tariff shock lessons shape policy – RBC
articles.expand: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) analysts review how Canada has weathered one year of U.S. tariff shocks. They note that Canada’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) and unemployment held up, but sectoral and regional damage was significant.

articles.title: Fed Minutes to offer insights into March hold decision amid hawkish outlook
articles.expand: The Federal Reserve (Fed) will publish its Minutes from the March 18 meeting on Wednesday. The release should be less about the decision itself and more about the officials’ “no rush to cut” narrative.

articles.title: EUR/USD Soars on Middle East Pause
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex EUR/USD rose sharply midweek to 1.1675, reaching a four-week high. Pressure on the US dollar came after President Donald Trump postponed the threat of strikes on Iranian civilian infrastructure for two weeks. The politician described this as a “bilateral ceasefire” conditional upon the reopening of the...

articles.title: Ceasefire Resets Fed Outlook, Markets Set to Look Through FOMC Minutes and Hot CPI
articles.expand: The ceasefire between the US and Iran has reset the Fed outlook, shifting market focus away from near-term inflation data toward the broader policy path. With oil prices falling and supply risks easing, the markets could be willing to look through both FOMC minutes today and a likely elevated March CPI reading on Friday. The […] The post Ceas...

articles.title: The USDCAD is lower but it is seeing more of a bounce. What would worry the seller?
articles.expand: The USDCAD moved lower as cease-fire optimism out of the Middle East helped shift the broader market tone. The news weighed on the USD, while stocks pushed higher and yields moved lower, creating a more risk-on backdrop that pressured the pair to the downside. articles.title: Ceasefire angst is the name of the game now
articles.expand: The mood in markets is very positive, with emerging markets posting their best day since 2022. A ceasefire is a particularly large relief for small crude oil importing nations, particularly those ones that relied on Middle Eastern refineries.
